Title: Deciphering the molecular logic of WOX5 function in the root stem cell organizer

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential macronutrient and signaling molecule in roots
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: root cells; linked to nitrate uptake/signaling in the QC context
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: WOX5-activated CEPR2 contributes to restoration of QC activity and is associated with nitrate uptake signaling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in wild-type QC than in wox5-1 QC based on reporter and transcriptome data
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: QC reporter signal reduced in wox5-1; direct WOX5-activated target in 5-day-old root QC
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard seedling growth; gene known from prior work to respond in nitrogen starvation signaling, but that treatment was not applied here
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pCEPR2 reporter; pWOX5 for misexpression in QC
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: FANS RNA-seq; chromatin association as direct WOX5 target with increased H3K4me3 and accessibility; GFP reporter; pWOX5-driven misexpression rescue of QC184:GUS in wox5-1
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate to engineer root nutrient uptake signaling and QC robustness, though evidence here is developmental and cell-type specific
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Selected by authors from the nitrate transport GO term; described as a paradoxical target with low basal expression in QC despite association with mature/root transport programs
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CEPR2 is a directly activated WOX5 target. 2. It is linked to nitrate uptake signaling. 3. CEPR2 reporter activity is lower in wox5-1 QC than wild type. 4. QC-specific CEPR2 expression partially restores QC184:GUS signal in wox5-1.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; CUT&RUN; ATAC-seq within BioProject PRJNA1127817/PRJNA1132792

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: WOX5-activated ERD14 partially restores QC activity in wox5-1
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in wild-type QC than in wox5-1 QC
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: QC reporter signal reduced in wox5-1
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Associated with osmotic stress, desiccation and cold responses
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pERD14 reporter; pWOX5 for misexpression in QC
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: FANS RNA-seq; reporter assay; pWOX5-driven misexpression rescue of QC184:GUS in wox5-1
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for stress-resilient root stem cell function, but not a direct nutrient metabolite gene
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a functionally relevant WOX5 target though not directly nutrient-metabolism related
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ERD14 is upregulated by WOX5 in QC. 2. Reporter signal is reduced in wox5-1. 3. QC-specific ERD14 expression partially rescues QC activity reporter in wox5-1.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: WOX5-activated SGP1 partially restores QC activity in wox5-1
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in wild-type QC than in wox5-1 QC; mainly expressed in cells with limited or null mitotic activity according to prior work cited
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: QC reporter signal reduced in wox5-1
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pSGP1 reporter; pWOX5 for misexpression in QC
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: FANS RNA-seq; reporter assay; pWOX5-driven misexpression rescue of QC184:GUS in wox5-1
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible target to modulate QC activity, but not directly a nutrient metabolism gene
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally relevant downstream WOX5 target in rescue assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SGP1 is a WOX5-upregulated target in QC. 2. Reporter activity decreases in wox5-1. 3. QC-specific SGP1 expression partially restores QC184:GUS in wox5-1.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural support for root growth and cell wall integrity
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: WOX5 activates CESA1 expression, but QC-specific CESA1 expression does not restore QC activity in wox5-1
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in wild-type QC than in wox5-1 QC
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: QC reporter signal reduced in wox5-1
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pCESA1 reporter; pWOX5 for misexpression in QC
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: FANS RNA-seq; direct-target chromatin features; reporter assay; pWOX5-driven misexpression in wox5-1
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant to root cell wall traits rather than nutrient quality
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included by authors as one of four representative direct WOX5 targets; not nutrition-centered
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CESA1 is directly activated by WOX5. 2. Reporter expression is reduced in wox5-1. 3. Unlike CEPR2, ERD14 and SGP1, pWOX5:CESA1 does not rescue QC184:GUS in wox5-1.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential macronutrient for plant growth; also linked to signaling and possibly auxin-related stem cell niche maintenance in the root QC
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: root quiescent center cells; root tissues/vacuoles via downstream transport systems
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: WOX5 promotes nitrate transport-associated gene expression in the quiescent center and supports QC organizer activity
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: nitrate transport and nutrient-associated transcriptional priming in the root stem cell organizer
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation and transcriptional repression coupled to chromatin-state remodeling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CEPR2; NRT1.1; CLCa; CESA1; SGP1; ERD14; CDF4; CYCD3;3; KRP1; KRP2; SIM; BORI2; BBM; RGF8; QQS; HAN; TAA1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates CEPR2, nitrate transport-related genes, CESA1, SGP1, ERD14, BBM, RGF8, QQS, HAN, TAA1, KRP1, KRP2, SIM; represses CDF4, CYCD3;3, BORI2 and other differentiation/cell-cycle genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-defining organizer regulator that directly activates nitrate transport-associated genes and broader QC programs; decisive for maintaining QC molecular identity
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: root stem cell organizer context; external nitrate is discussed as affecting WOX5 function
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedback with nitrate is proposed in discussion but not directly proven in this study
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Previously reported interaction with TPL/HDA19 discussed; this study infers chromatin-related action but does not directly validate new complex composition
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 5-day-old seedlings grown on 0.5× MS agar without sucrose under standard long-day conditions; nitrate status discussed conceptually but not experimentally manipulated here
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: QC-specific expression in the root stem cell organizer
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pWOX5 (native QC-specific); p35S in WOX5-GR proxy line for binding analysis
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: WOX5-dependent increases or decreases in H3K9ac, H3K4me3, H3K27me3, and chromatin accessibility at target loci; promotes bivalent chromatin at a subset of directly activated targets
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: wox5-1 mutant versus wild type FANS RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; anti-WOX5 ChIP-chip using 35S:WOX5-GR proxy; CUT&RUN for histone marks; ATAC-seq; reporter assays; rescue/misexpression of downstream targets in wox5-1
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not established; broad pleiotropic effects on stem cell maintenance, differentiation, and cell-cycle regulation are likely if manipulated
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily fundamental regulator; could be leveraged to modulate root stem cell organizer function and nutrient-response architecture, but likely with major developmental pleiotropy
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Main study focus is stem cell organizer biology rather than nutritional quality. Nutrient-metabolism relevance is specifically through nitrate transport-associated pathways upregulated by WOX5 in QC cells
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WOX5 is the central experimentally studied regulator in QC cells. 2. It acts both as transcriptional repressor and activator, with most direct targets being activated. 3. WOX5 remodels chromatin states, altering H3K9ac, H3K4me3, H3K27me3 and accessibility. 4. A major unexpected WOX5 output is activation of nitrate transport-associated genes including CEPR2, NRT1.1 and CLCa. 5. Expression of CEPR2, ERD14 and SGP1 partially restores a WOX5 activity reporter in wox5-1, supporting functional relevance of downstream targets.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA1127817, PRJNA1132792); ATAC-seq (BioProject PRJNA1127817, PRJNA1132792); histone CUT&RUN (BioProject PRJNA1127817, PRJNA1132792); ChIP-chip (GEO GSE270459)
